what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry that describes the compiled/generated JavaScript bundle _ds_bundle.js for the ConceptV design system.
observations: It is a meta-annotation about _ds_bundle.js, not the bundle itself.
It records the bundle as a ~256 KB (~6970-line) generated JavaScript artifact.
It lists the @ds-bundle header fields: format:3, namespace ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c, a components[] array with names and sourcePaths, and a sourceHashes map.

It enumerates leaf components (Avatar, Badge, Button, Card, Glyphic, Input, Modal, Segmented, Stepper, Switch, Tabs) and core engine exports (ContainmentTree, DiagramSolver, RenderType, CoreTypes, Slide, Archetypes).
It notes that some exports share source files, e.g., RenderType + CoreTypes from core/RenderType.jsx and Slide + Archetypes from core/Slide.jsx.
It carries a 'never hand-edit' warning and a 'stale mid-turn' caveat from the consuming project's charter.
It contains no extracted code symbols.
standouts: The described bundle embeds a machine-readable @ds-bundle JSON header with content-addressed sourceHashes for every source file.
The bundle packs both leaf UI components and the core generative engine into one runtime artifact.
The interpretive entry explicitly marks the artifact as generated output that should not be hand-edited and may be stale.
conventions: Generated artifacts are treated as read-only; truth lives in the listed source files.
Provenance is recorded via content hashes in the bundle header.
Single-bundle distribution: one packed file serves components and engine.
Interpretive layer uses a structured schema with neutral, file-local claims.
concerns: The entry makes claims about another file's build process and staleness that are not verifiable from this JSON alone.
No code symbols are present, so structural relationships must be inferred from the textual summary.
Because the bundle is described as stale mid-turn, this interpretive snapshot may already misrepresent the current source state.
notes: A neutral interpretive snapshot of a generated, packed design-system bundle, emphasizing its self-describing provenance header and the do-not-edit/stale-output caveats.
questions: Which build step emits the @ds-bundle format:3 header and sourceHashes?
How is the bundle's staleness checked against the header at runtime or build time?
Are CoreTypes and Archetypes re-exports or distinct entities co-located with RenderType and Slide?
What is the exact relationship between this .json interpretive file and the .js bundle it describes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a structured interpretive summary of the generated bundle, exactly as its fields indicate.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a neutral, machine-readable interpretive record of a compiled design-system artifact, capturing its contents, provenance, and caveats so downstream tooling can treat the bundle as generated output and detect drift from its source files.
novelty: The combination of a generated-bundle provenance manifest with an explicit 'stale mid-turn' interpretive caveat, packaged in a structured ledger entry rather than a plain code comment, is unusual.
contribution: It contributes a structured, file-local interpretive summary and provenance/staleness signals about the generated bundle that a fused system can keep and query without parsing the compiled JavaScript blob.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger JSON for the compiled ConceptV design-system bundle, recording its @ds-bundle manifest, exported components, sourceHashes provenance, and generated/stale-output caveats.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral, file-local interpretive summary of the generated bundle so fusion tooling can understand its contents and provenance without reading the compiled artifact.
